What is ESG Assurance?

ESG Assurance is an independent verification process that confirms the accuracy, credibility, and completeness of your sustainability disclosures, whether it’s your Greenhouse Gas (GHG) inventory, Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR), or Annual Sustainability Report (ASR).

Using globally recognized standards like the AA1000 Standard (AA1000AS v3), third-party validates your data and builds stakeholder confidence. It also prepares your organization for regulatory scrutiny, investor evaluation, and global sustainability rankings. At Growlity, we offer independent assurance services tailored to your sustainability reporting needs, delivering credibility, transparency, and audit readiness across your journey.

Understanding

CBAM Timeline: Key Milestones

Environment

Social

Governance

Environment

We measure and report your environmental footprint across key impact areas.

  • Carbon & Energy – Track GHG emissions, Scope 1, 2 & 3, and renewable energy adoption.
  • Resource Efficiency – Monitor waste generation, water usage, and recycling practices.
  • Ecosystem Impact – Assess land use, biodiversity protection, and climate adaptation.

FAQs

On The Side of Ambitious

 Action 

We help brands stand out and grow by creating innovative, results-driven strategies that engage customers and drive success.

What is ESG Reporting and why is it needed?

ESG reporting is the structured disclosure of your company’s Environmental, Social, and Governance performance. It goes beyond traditional financial reporting to reflect how your business manages sustainability-related risks and opportunities. It is essential for improving transparency, meeting regulatory and stakeholder expectations, and building trust with investors, customers, and regulators. A strong ESG report also supports better decision-making, reputation management, and long-term resilience in a rapidly evolving business landscape.

What ESG framework should I follow?

The right ESG framework depends on your industry, reporting objectives, stakeholder expectations, and regulatory obligations. Companies aiming for comprehensive stakeholder communication often choose the Global Reporting Initiative (GRI), while those targeting investor-specific disclosures may opt for SASB. Climate-related risk reporting aligns well with TCFD, and companies operating in or linked to the EU must consider CSRD requirements. In India, listed companies follow SEBI’s BRSR framework. At Growlity, we help you navigate these options and design a reporting approach that integrates the most relevant standards—ensuring both compliance and impact.

Is ESG Reporting mandatory in India?

Yes. ESG reporting is mandatory for the top 1000 listed companies in India under SEBI’s Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR) mandate. However, ESG is gaining traction beyond listed companies. SMEs, exporters, and supply chain partners are increasingly adopting ESG voluntarily to meet client requirements, improve credit access, and prepare for global market expectations like CBAM, EU Taxonomy, and investor ratings.

Can you help with ESG ratings like CDP, MSCI, or EcoVadis?

Absolutely. We provide complete ESG rating support—covering readiness assessment, gap analysis, data collection, evidence mapping, and final submission. Our expertise spans multiple ratings and disclosures including CDP, EcoVadis, MSCI, Sustainalytics, S&P Global, and others. We help you boost scores, close compliance gaps, and enhance your ESG positioning across global supply chains and investor platforms.

How is ESG different from CSR or SDG Reporting?

While CSR (Corporate Social Responsibility) often focuses on voluntary, philanthropic efforts, ESG is performance-based, strategic, and integrated into business operations. SDG (Sustainable Development Goal) reporting aligns your impact with the 17 UN Global Goals, but ESG reporting goes deeper into how environmental and social issues affect your business and vice versa. ESG captures both risk and opportunity, providing a roadmap for long-term value creation and sustainable growth.

Explore More

You might also Be Interested In…!!!

CDP Reporting

CDP is a globally recognized environmental disclosure system that enables organizations to report their carbon emissions, climate-related risks, and environmental performance. Trusted by thousands of investors, companies, and cities, CDP plays a critical role in driving climate transparency and accountability. As a CDP consultant and disclosure partner, Growlity empowers organizations to navigate the full CDP reporting framework, enhance data quality, and improve scores. Our expert support ensures climate compliance, strengthens sustainability disclosures, and reinforces stakeholder trust—positioning your business for credible, low-carbon growth.

Read More

CDP Reporting

RJC Certification

The Responsible Jewellery Council (RJC) is a globally recognized organization that sets standards for the jewellery, gemstone, and watch industry. It promotes responsible business practices, including ethics, human rights, social responsibility, and environmental stewardship across the entire supply chain, from mine to retail. RJC certification demonstrates a company’s commitment to integrity, transparency, and sustainability within the jewellery sector. Being RJC certified builds trust among consumers, stakeholders, and business partners, ensuring accountability and long-term value in all aspects of the jewellery industry.

Read More

RJC Certification

Net Zero & Decarbonisation

Net Zero is no longer a distant ambition, it’s a business-critical commitment. At Growlity, we help organizations chart their Net Zero transformation with robust GHG accounting, practical decarbonisation strategies, and alignment with frameworks like the GHG Protocol, ISO 14064, SBTi, and CDP. Whether you’re just beginning your decarbonisation journey or already setting net zero targets, our sustainability consultants deliver tailored solutions that are strategic, science-based, and results-driven.
